semantic.role: Type definitions for database record shapes consumed by the repository data-access layer.
responsibilities: Define the FileRecord interface specifying the structure for indexed file metadata (path, hash, kind, timestamps, docs, authority status).
Define the FileStatusRecord interface for tracking file status during indexing (hash, kind, modification time).
Define the FileCommitRecord interface for associating file changes with commits.
Define the DriftEvent interface for recording semantic drift occurrences.
Define the UsageSummaryRow and UsageTotals interfaces for aggregating symbol usage statistics.
Define the ViolationRecord interface for representing architectural rule violations.
Define the GlobalStatus interface for capturing aggregate system state.
outOfScope: Data access methods (queries, inserts, updates) — this file only provides type contracts.
API or CLI surface — pure internal data layer.
invariants: Infra repositories must not import from deep implementation paths when a facade exists — this module imports from src/types/protocol.js which is a public types facade, satisfying the rule.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Provide stable, reusable type definitions for the database record layer, ensuring type safety and consistency across repository implementations.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Part of the v1.0.0 release of ArchSpine establishing the initial record schema contracts.

publicSurface.description: Describes a file entry stored in the index: contains path, hash, file kind, last indexed timestamp, documentation text, and an authoritative flag. Used by file repository implementations for read/write operations.
Captures the current indexing status of a file: hash, kind, and modification time. Consumed by the scanner to decide whether re-indexing is required.
Associates a file with a commit identifier. Used by change tracking and post-commit derivation logic.
Represents a detected semantic drift event with relevant metadata. Employed by the drift detection engine and drift repository.
A row in the usage summary aggregating per-symbol usage counts. Consumed by the usage engine and reporting views.
Aggregate totals of symbol usage across the codebase. Used by the usage engine and dashboard summaries.
Stores a single architectural rule violation with context. Used by the check engine, violation repository, and view generators.
Global aggregate status of the repository indexing, including health indicators. Used by the health view and administrative services.
